food_name: Gatorade Thirst Quencher Orange (500ml bottle, Singapore market)
aliases: Gatorade Thirst Quencher (500ml bottle — Orange flavour, Singapore market, PepsiCo)

english: Gatorade Thirst Quencher (500ml bottle — Orange flavour, Singapore market, PepsiCo)

region_of_origin: USA — Gatorade Company (PepsiCo division). Originally developed at the University of Florida in 1965.
food_subgroup: Isotonic sports drink; 500ml PET bottle; carbohydrate-electrolyte beverage; sucrose, glucose, dextrose blend.

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn URI exists for Gatorade branded sports drink; closest parent is sports/isotonic beverage category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for branded isotonic sports drink.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is 1 bottle = 500ml (approximately 500g), providing 130 kcal, 34g sugar, and 440mg sodium.

nutrient_source.title: Gatorade Thirst Quencher Orange — USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods (FDC ID 1101289) and PepsiCo Singapore product label
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods; PepsiCo Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Gatorade Thirst Quencher Orange (FDC ID 1101289), cross-referenced against PepsiCo Singapore 500ml bottle label. Values are per 100g (100ml) of product. Energy 26 kcal, carbohydrate 6.8g, sugar 6.8g, sodium 88mg, potassium 25mg confirmed from both sources. Protein and fat are confirmed zero by product formulation — this is a carbohydrate-electrolyte beverage containing no protein or fat ingredients.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Sports Drinks — University of Sydney GI Database; Gatorade (sucrose + glucose + dextrose blend) GI ~78
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of approximately 78 (high) sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for Gatorade sports drink. The sucrose, glucose, and dextrose blend in Gatorade produces rapid gastric emptying and fast intestinal glucose absorption, consistent with a high-GI classification. GL per 500ml serving calculated as (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (78 x 34) / 100 = 26.52, rounded to 26.5.

hypertension.guidance: A single 500ml bottle of Gatorade delivers 440mg sodium — approximately 22% of the recommended 2,000mg/day sodium limit for hypertensive patients; clinicians should counsel patients that Gatorade is not a low-sodium beverage and should not be consumed as a routine hydration choice when managing hypertension, particularly in the context of Singapore's already high-sodium hawker diet.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI 78 (high); 34g sugar per 500ml bottle; GL 26.5 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Gatorade has a high GI of 78 and delivers 34g of rapidly absorbed sugars (equivalent to 8.5 teaspoons) per 500ml bottle, producing a glycaemic load of 26.5 per serving; T2DM patients must be counselled that Gatorade's sports marketing creates a misleading 'healthy drink' perception and that for sedentary individuals it functions as a high-GI sugar-sweetened beverage equivalent to regular soft drinks — G2 (Gatorade Low Calorie) with approximately 80% less sugar is a more appropriate alternative if electrolyte replacement is genuinely needed.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 440mg per 500ml; zero saturated fat but high sugar load
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While Gatorade contains no saturated fat or cholesterol, the high sodium content (440mg per bottle) and high sugar load (34g per bottle) are relevant cardiovascular risk factors; regular consumption by sedentary individuals may contribute to elevated blood pressure and hypertriglyceridaemia, and clinicians should advise CVD patients to limit Gatorade to genuine post-exercise electrolyte replacement contexts only.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Gatorade Thirst Quencher Orange (500ml PET bottle, PepsiCo Singapore) is the world's most widely recognised isotonic sports drink, originally developed at the University of Florida in 1965 and now sold globally. Per 500ml bottle: 130 kcal, 34g total sugar (sucrose + glucose + dextrose blend), 440mg sodium, 125mg potassium, GI approximately 78 (high), glycaemic load 26.5. The carbohydrate-electrolyte formulation is specifically engineered for rapid gastric emptying and fast intestinal glucose absorption to replenish muscle glycogen and electrolytes during sustained intense exercise. Protein and fat content are zero by product formulation — this is a pure carbohydrate-electrolyte beverage. A critical clinical counselling priority in Singapore: T2DM patients and the general public frequently consume Gatorade as a perceived 'health drink' or 'better than Coke' option due to its global sports marketing. The clinical reality is that for sedentary individuals, Gatorade is a high-GI (78), high-GL (26.5) sugar-sweetened beverage with the same metabolic concern level as regular carbonated soft drinks. G2 (Gatorade Low Calorie / Reduced Sugar) provides the same electrolyte profile with approximately 80% less sugar and is a meaningfully better alternative for non-athletes. Gatorade is halal-certified, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods (exact match for Gatorade Thirst Quencher Orange) and cross-referenced against PepsiCo Singapore product label. GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database. All core macros confirmed from approved primary sources.
notes: Sports drink; protein_g is naturally zero as this is a carbohydrate-electrolyte beverage containing no protein ingredients by formulation. Fat_g is naturally zero by product formulation — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are -1 (not reported by source — not applicable for a fat-free beverage). water_g is -1 (not reported by USDA Branded source for this product; estimated ~93g/100g based on beverage composition but not confirmed by source, so -1 is used per numeric hygiene rules). Dietary fibre is 0.0 confirmed. Processing level corrected to ultra-processed consistent with NOVA classification for branded formulated beverages.
